title: magnolia fordiana var. fordiana
intro: Magnolia fordiana var. fordiana, also known as the Bornean magnolia or Raintree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Magnoliaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The bark, flowers, and leaves of the plant have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, with reported health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and relief from pain and discomfort. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and ointments, and is also used as a natural insect repellent and anti-diarrheal agent.
